chunk_summary: This section discusses dietary modifications to manage symptoms in conditions like lactose intolerance, celiac disease, and cow’s milk allergy, emphasizing symptom-trigger restriction, nutritional replacement, and growth maintenance.
learning_objectives: Understand the principles of dietary modification for symptom management in food-related conditions.
Learn how to replace calories and protein to prevent growth delays during dietary restrictions.
Review updated guidelines for diagnosing pediatric celiac disease and managing cow’s milk allergy.
key_facts: Dietary modifications should restrict only symptom-triggering foods and for the shortest duration possible.
Nutritional replacement, including calories and protein, is essential to prevent growth delays in children.
The ESPGHAN 2020 guidelines provide updated diagnostic criteria for pediatric celiac disease.
The World Allergy Organization DRACMA guidelines (2024) offer updated recommendations for managing cow’s milk allergy.
Lactose intolerance management involves avoiding lactose-containing foods while ensuring adequate calcium intake.
reference_quote: We will restrict only what triggers symptoms, for the shortest time, and we will replace calories and protein so growth does not slow.
